Most back pain isn't serious, and that's a fact.  But there is a minority of cases that are serious warning signs that many people ignore because they think it's just fussiness or lack of exercise.  Today I'm going to show you five signs that, if you have them, mean you need to see a doctor as soon as possible.  Not tomorrow, not next week, as soon as possible .  Save this video and send it to someone who complains of back pain but doesn't go to a medium.  A pain signal that wakes you up in the middle of the night.  Mechanical pain, that is, pain caused by posture, exertion, or a herniated disc, usually improves with rest.  When pain wakes you up in the middle of the night for no apparent reason, it may indicate something inflammatory or infectious, and in some rare cases, even a tumor.  So, don't ignore this sign.  Sign number two, weakness in the leg or arm.  If you start to feel your leg giving way, that you stumble for no apparent reason, or that you can no longer grip objects with the same strength as before, this is a sign of neurological impairment, and the herniated disc may be severely compressing a nerve. This is a sign of urgency. Sign three, loss of bladder or bowel control.  This is the most serious sign of all.  If you start having difficulty urinating, or conversely, are unable to hold your urine or even control your bowel movements, this could be what is called cauda equina syndrome.  It is a surgical medical emergency .  So, in these cases, the patient should go immediately to an emergency room , as it is usually a more serious case, one that typically requires immediate surgical treatment .  Sign four, pain that is accompanied by fever.  So, fever with back pain can indicate an infection, including in the disc itself, which we call the disc.  It is a serious condition that requires urgent diagnosis and treatment.  So, don't ignore signs of fever and see a doctor.  Sign number five, pain after trauma or a fall.  It seems obvious, right, that we should talk about seeking help after a fall or trauma, but many people fall, hit their back, and think it will go away.  If there has been trauma and the pain persists or is only getting worse, it is necessary to go to an emergency room to rule out a vertebral fracture, especially in elderly patients and those with osteoporosis.  These are the five warning signs.  If you have any of them, don't wait. Seek medical attention immediately.  The back pain from everyday life, the kind you see from your posture, from sitting for too long, that pain that makes you think, "Oh, I've been sedentary for too long," that pain has treatment and can improve.  But these signs that I just showed you, that I just talked about in the video, they need an urgent evaluation.  Subscribe to the channel, leave a like, and tell me in the comments if you have or have had any of these signs.  Until the next video.
